meta.pricingNote: Sophia is subscription based (billed monthly). Confirm current pricing at sophia.org before budgeting — the default used by the calculator is editable in Settings.
meta.dataNote: VERIFY BEFORE PURCHASING: The official WGU Transfer Pathway Agreement PDF was not available when this list was compiled. Statuses below are labeled 'Pathway-listed (verify)', 'Community-reported (verify)', 'Needs manual review', or 'Unlikely to apply'. Nothing here is a guarantee of transfer credit.

courses.name: English Composition I
courses.notes: Touchstone essay assignments graded by Sophia evaluators (allow 1–3 days per grade). Widely listed on the WGU Teachers College pathway — verify code for your catalog year.
courses.tips: Write the essays back-to-back and submit early; grading time is the bottleneck, not the work.

courses.name: U.S. History I
courses.notes: Multiple-choice challenges and milestones, no proctored final.
courses.tips: Keep a one-page timeline of eras as you go — the final milestone pulls from everything.

courses.name: U.S. Government
courses.wguEquivalent: American Politics and the US Constitution (C963)
courses.notes: Community-reported equivalency — confirm this course appears on your official pathway before relying on it.
courses.tips: C963's OA at WGU is considered tough; clearing it via transfer is a popular move if your agreement allows it.

courses.name: Introduction to Statistics
courses.notes: Widely listed on WGU pathways. Calculator-friendly, formula sheet provided.
courses.tips: Do every practice challenge twice; the milestones reuse question styles.

courses.name: College Algebra
courses.wguEquivalent: General math requirement — NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW
courses.notes: BAELED's math requirements are largely program-specific (Mathematics for Elementary Educators sequence is taken at WGU). Confirm whether College Algebra applies to your plan before spending time on it.
courses.tips: If it doesn't map to your plan, skip it — spend the time on Statistics instead.

courses.name: Ancient Greek Philosophers
courses.notes: One of Sophia's quickest courses. Community-reported humanities match — verify.
courses.tips: Frequently finished in a weekend. Great confidence-builder as a first course.

courses.name: Art History I
courses.notes: Alternative humanities option. Only one humanities transfer will apply — don't take both this and Ancient Greek Philosophers.
courses.tips: Pick whichever humanities course interests her more; only one counts.

courses.name: Public Speaking
courses.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — requires recorded speech submissions. Confirm it maps to D268 in your agreement before starting.
courses.tips: Record all speeches in one session once outlines are approved.

courses.name: Communication at Work
courses.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — some pathways list a different Sophia communication course. Only one communication transfer will apply.
courses.tips: No speeches required, making it faster than Public Speaking if it counts.

courses.name: Human Biology (+ Lab)
courses.notes: Community-reported: a Sophia lab science has satisfied the natural-science-with-lab requirement. Verify which lab course your agreement lists.
courses.tips: The lab component is virtual — screenshot results as you go for the write-ups.

courses.name: Environmental Science
courses.notes: Easy course, but unclear it maps to any BAELED science slot. Review before taking.
courses.tips: Only worth it if your evaluation confirms a slot for it.

courses.name: Introduction to Chemistry
courses.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — physical science equivalencies are inconsistently reported. C165 at WGU is also considered manageable, so transfer may not be worth it.
courses.tips: If unverified, consider just taking C165 at WGU during term 1.

courses.name: Introduction to Psychology
courses.notes: UNLIKELY TO APPLY — BAELED's psychology content (D094) sits in the professional core, which generally must be completed at WGU.
courses.tips: Skip unless your evaluation shows an open elective slot.

courses.name: Lifespan Development
courses.notes: UNLIKELY TO APPLY — development content is covered by professional core courses taken at WGU.

courses.name: English Composition II
courses.notes: UNLIKELY TO APPLY — BAELED typically requires only one composition course.
courses.tips: Skip unless your evaluation says otherwise.
